topic: Review of search and retrieval code for system-spec-kit MCP server

findingDetails.title: Summary embedding search ranks only an arbitrary capped prefix
Summary lane uses weaker active/scope filtering than the primary retrieval lanes

results.summary: The scope specifically requested search/retrieval numeric correctness, scope-then-limit, and recall recovery review. Two summary-channel defects were confirmed.
The review scope has no checklist.md; this fan-out lineage treated the audit scope spec as the governing artifact.

packets.claim: querySummaryEmbeddings computes top-K summary matches from only an arbitrary capped prefix of memory_summaries, so high-similarity rows outside that prefix cannot be returned.
packets.counterevidenceSought: Checked the caller in stage1-candidate-gen.ts and the summary module for a later corpus-wide rerank, scoped prefilter, ORDER BY, active projection join, or vector-index nearest-neighbor path; none is present before the LIMIT.
packets.alternativeExplanation: The LIMIT is an intentional hot-path performance guard. That does not preserve top-K semantics because the cosine sort happens after the capped read rather than before or through an indexed nearest-neighbor query.
packets.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the summary channel scopes to eligible rows and computes nearest neighbors over the complete eligible set before applying the caller limit.

transitions.reason: Initial discovery with direct code evidence

packets.claim: Stage 1 rehydrates summary-channel IDs from memory_index without active_memory_projection and then applies exact spec_folder equality, giving the summary lane different active/subtree semantics from vector and FTS retrieval.
packets.counterevidenceSought: Compared summary hydration and folder filtering against vector and FTS code paths. Vector and FTS use active projection/subtree predicates before returning rows; summary hydration does not, and its local folder filter is exact equality only.
packets.alternativeExplanation: Later response formatting may drop some non-canonical rows, but it cannot restore descendant summary hits removed by exact equality or guarantee inactive IDs never affect scoring/telemetry before formatting.
packets.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if summary hydration joins active_memory_projection and uses the same specFolder subtree predicate as vector/FTS before scoring or before adding candidates.

transitions.reason: Initial discovery with direct code evidence
